definitions: An intergovernmental organization established in the framework of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) in 1974 after the 1973 oil crisis.
It acts as a policy adviser to its member states, working to ensure reliable, affordable and clean energy for its 31 member countries and beyond.
examples: The International Energy Agency released its latest report on global oil demand.
Ministers from the International Energy Agency discussed strategies for energy security.
The International Energy Agency plays a crucial role in shaping international energy policy.
